kind.description: Executor selection. Kinds starting with "codex" run as codex exec subagents, kinds starting with "claude" as claude -p subagents, the exact kind "acp_opencode" as an opencode worker driven over ACP (other acp* values are rejected at plan time), and other kinds as deterministic local tasks.

prompt_template.description: Worker instructions. May embed template references: {{objective}}, {{tasks.<taskId>.result.<dotpath>}} (producer must be in the task's depends_on transitive closure; the dotpath must resolve against the producer's synthesized worker result schema, must not path into arrays, and must not resolve to a nullable ["<scalar>","null"] property), {{item...}} (only with foreach), and {{gate_feedback}} (only with gates). All references are statically validated at plan time; leftover {{inputs.*}} references are rejected (saved-workflow inputs are expanded before validation). Template syntax ({{) is forbidden everywhere else, including gates[].command argv entries.

output_schema.description: Restricted typed-output declaration (v2 subset): whitelist keywords {type, properties, items, enum, description, title}; root must be type object; nesting depth <= 4, <= 64 total properties, <= 8 nullable unions, <= 32KB serialized. The runner generates required (all property names) and injects additionalProperties: false on every object; the planned spec stores the normalized form. Not supported for local task kinds.

gates.description: Quality gates executed sequentially in declaration order after an accepted (schema-valid, succeeded) worker result, while the task holds its concurrency slot. All gates exit 0 -> the task succeeds; any non-zero exit or timeout -> status gate_failed, which is retryable under the task's single retry_policy.max_attempts counter (worker failures and gate failures share attempts). Gates run with no OS sandbox, cwd = workspace_policy.workspace_root, an env allowlist of PATH/HOME/TMPDIR/LANG/LC_ALL, argv-only (no shell). Not supported for local task kinds.

gate_feedback_tail_bytes.description: Per-stream tail size (bytes) of failed-gate stdout/stderr included in the {{gate_feedback}} retry text. Default 4096; requires gates.

route.description: Enum-based branching (F4). The worker envelope gains a required `route` string enum (value domain = values). When the routing task finally succeeds (post-gates) the route resolves once: cases[value] (falling back to default) selects the case tasks to activate; case tasks not selected become skipped_by_route (terminal, satisfies dependencies, never fails the run, not resumable). Plan-time rules: cases keys must be a subset of values and default is required (V1); every case task must include the routing task in its depends_on transitive closure (V2); only case tasks of the same route may depend directly on a case task (V3); a task may be a case task of at most one routing task (V4); route and foreach are mutually exclusive and the routing task must not list itself (V5); template references to a case-task producer require the consumer to be a co-activated case task of the same route (V7). Not supported for local task kinds.

foreach.description: Bounded fan-out (F6). When the task's dependencies are satisfied it expands instead of launching a worker (status `expanded`, non-terminal, no attempt consumed): the resolved items array yields children `<task_id>.<index>` (0-based) that inherit kind/model/effort/profile/timeout_ms/retry_policy/output_schema/gates/gate_feedback_tail_bytes and render the parent's prompt_template with {{item}}/{{item.<path>}}. One replayable `tasks_expanded` event records the expansion (fail-closed when the serialized items exceed 256 KiB). More items than max_items fails the parent without truncation; zero items succeed immediately with an empty aggregate. After all children are terminal the runner synthesizes the parent's result.json (typed envelope, attempt_id "aggregate") with output.results as an order-preserving array of {index, task_id, status, output|null}; the parent succeeds while permanently failed children stay within tolerated_failure_count. Downstream templates may reference only {{tasks.<task_id>.result.output.results}}. ID-collision rule: any other spec task_id matching ^<task_id>\.[0-9]+$ is rejected at plan time (the child id space is reserved). Budget precondition: spec task count + sum of max_items over all foreach tasks must not exceed max_agents, or the plan is rejected. route and foreach are mutually exclusive. Not supported for local task kinds.

model.description: Worker model id. Optional for codex and claude tasks; REQUIRED for acp_opencode tasks, where it is an ACP model id (e.g. "openrouter/anthropic/claude-haiku-4.5") fixed per session via session/set_model.

command.description: Argv array executed directly (no shell). Elements must not contain control characters or template syntax ({{); command[0] (the executable) must not start with "-" (later elements may be flags). Each element is at most 512 characters.

timeout_ms.description: Required per-gate timeout. Effective timeout is max(min(timeout_ms, remaining run duration budget), 1000); on expiry the gate's process group is killed and the verdict records timed_out.

values.description: Distinct enum values the worker may report as `route` (limits aligned with the output_schema enum limits).

cases.description: Maps a route value to the case tasks activated by that resolution. Keys must be a subset of values; values without a key resolve to default. An empty array is allowed (the resolution activates nothing).

default.description: Required fallback selection for values without an explicit case. An empty array is allowed.

items.description: Exactly one whole-field {{tasks.<id>.result.<dotpath>}} reference (no surrounding text). The producer must be in this task's depends_on transitive closure and the dotpath must resolve to an array-typed property in the producer's synthesized worker result schema (for a foreach producer, only output.results).

max_items.description: Required hard bound on the expansion. More resolved items than max_items fails the parent (fail-closed, no truncation). Counts toward the plan-time budget precondition: spec tasks + sum of max_items <= max_agents.

concurrency.description: Per-parent child concurrency; the effective limit is min(concurrency, workflow.max_concurrency). Defaults to workflow.max_concurrency.

tolerated_failure_count.description: Number of permanently failed children (after retries) the parent tolerates while still folding to succeeded. Default 0.

required.description: Runner-generated (all property names); user-authored values are rejected at plan time.

additionalProperties.description: Runner-injected on every object; user-authored values are rejected at plan time.
